Video.summary: Tonight's episode features another installment of "The Family", and Tim Conway's "World's Oldest Man".
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Tim Conway

Video.summary: Carol struggles with "Easy Open" packaging, Harvey & Tim spoof Jacques Cousteau, and Carol & Vicki are torn.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Guest Stars Mel Torme, Nanette Fabray, and Don Rickles pitch in to help tell "The Tin Pan Alley Story".
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles

Image.alt: Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles
Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles
Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles
Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles
Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles
Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles
Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles
Nanette Fabray, Mel Torme, Don Rickles

Video.summary: Sammy Davis, Jr. stars as a flamboyant galley slave, then appears before the Queen & King as a debt collector.
Video.title: Sammy Davis Jr.

Video.summary: Paul Sand guests! Sketches dealing with weddings are featured. Also: bits related to the energy crisis.
Video.title: Paul Sand

Video.summary: Dick Martin is the town gigolo on "As The Stomach Turns." Also: a spoof of "Sunset Boulevard."
Video.title: Steve Lawrence, Dick Martin

Video.summary: Carol's guests tonight are Garry Moore and Durward Kirby.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Garry Moore, Durward Kirby

Video.summary: Don Adams plays a talk show host beset with technical difficulties and Carol's agitated husband.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Lesley Ann Warren, Don Adams

Video.summary: Guest star Steve Lawrence takes the stage to sing a duet with Dick.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Vincent Price and Eydie Gorme star. Features an encounter on an elevator & the movie spoof "House Of Terror."
Video.title: Eydie Gorme, Vincent Price

Video.summary: William Conrad & Isabel Sanford star! Short spoofs highlight this episode, which also features "Carol & Sis."
Video.title: William Conrad, Isabel Sanford

Video.summary: Guest James Coco takes a slow boat to romance in the Saturday Night Tearjerker movie spoof, "One Way Ticket."
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: James Coco, Pointer Sister

Video.summary: Nanette Fabray stars as Carol's rival. Also, a "V.I.P." interview and a satiric look at foreign airlines.
Video.title: Nanette Fabray

Video.summary: Diahann Carroll and Carol perform a number. The Smothers Brothers drop by, and "Carol & Sis" try astrology.
Video.title: Diahann Carroll, Smothers Brothers

Video.summary: Guest John Byner plays both a villain and a trial witness. On "Carol & Sis", The Bradfords join a group.
Video.title: John Byner

Video.summary: Nanette Fabray appears on "As The Stomach Turns." Also featured: "Carol & Sis" go gaga over Roger's briefcase.
Video.title: Nanette Fabray

Video.summary: Steve Lawrence and Carol Channing join Carol, Harvey, Vicki, and Lyle for a tribute to the Academy Awards.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Carol Channing, Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Alan King & Lena Zavaroni join Carol for sketches set on Hollywood Boulevard, a baseball game, and a bus stop.
Video.title: Alan King, Lena Zavaroni

Video.summary: Jack Klugman stars as a man annoyed by his sister-in-law. Also: Carol, Tim and Harvey get a little work done.
Video.title: Jack Klugman

Video.summary: Don Rickles guests as a house painter on this "Carol & Sis" sketch. Harvey and Carol face a home invasion.
Video.title: Don Rickles

Video.summary: Jonathan Winters crashes Q&A. Sid Caesar teaches Carol self-defense, then joins the gang for a spoof.
Video.title: Jonathan Winters, Sid Caesar

Video.summary: Lucille Ball and Mel Torme spoof the classic comedy "Some Like It Hot" with a gender-flipping twist.
Video.title: Lucille Ball, Mel Torme

Video.summary: Kitchen commercials highlight this episode, and Tim returns as the steelworker looking to do away with Carol.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: Carol's guests are George Chakiris and Shirley Jones!
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Shirley Jones, George Chakiris

Video.summary: Tonight's guest: Mr. Steve Lawrence!
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Tim Conway & Steve Lawrence appear in commercial spoofs. Tim plays The Oldest Man and is in a pantomime.
Video.title: Tim Conway, Steve Lawrence

Video.summary: Jack Klugman plays a skeptical man annoyed by his sister-in-law's visiting date--a clairvoyant.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Best Of The Carol Burnett Show
Video.title: Jack Klugman

Video.summary: Bernadette Peters and Cass Elliot guest star in "As The Stomach Turns", and Lyle causes havoc and absurdity.
Video.title: Bernadette Peters, Cass Elliot

Video.summary: Ms. Betty White appears in "The Family." Also: Carol & Tim try to get rid of a counterfeit bill.
Video.title: Betty White

Video.summary: Features Harvey & Carol encounter a wishing well, and another installment of "The Family."
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: George Chakiris and Shirley Jones guest. Chakiris joins for Q&A, while Jones is a secretary in "Carol & Sis."
Video.title: Shirley Jones, George Chakiris

Video.summary: Scoey Mitchell is Carol's guest. Featuring "The Queen Elizabeth Show", a Q&A and a "Queen & Sis" sketch.
Video.title: Scoey Mitchell

Video.summary: Jim Nabors guests. Features Harvey's Emmy Award speech, and a presentation of "Hollywood Canteen."
Video.title: Jim Nabors

Video.summary: Nancy Wilson and Nanette Fabray star in "As The Stomach Turns." On "Carol & Sis", siblings practice First Aid.
Video.title: Nancy Wilson, Nanette Fabray

Video.summary: Step back in time with "The Family" as Eunice thinks back to her early days. Also, Tim plays a palace guard.
Video.title: Family Show

Video.summary: David Frost and Marilyn Horne star. Frost plays a Rolls Royce dealer, while Horne takes the musical spotlight.
Video.title: Marilyn Horne, David Frost
